title: PoliticsMeet the New Proud BoysThe far-right group's views and tactics are now emulated by federal agents.By Ali BrelandIllustration by The Atlantic
raw_response: {"rating": -5.5, "lean": "Left", "explanation": "This is a news report/analysis piece. It leans Left because the author repeatedly equates DHS/ICE actions with extremist militias, uses loaded descriptors to condemn federal enforcement, and advances a thesis that the Trump administration has effectively formed an \"in-house militia\" to harass opponents. The consistency of these value-laden framings, with limited balancing context, supports a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline and dek frame federal agents as emulating a \"far-right\" group, establishing a condemnatory premise before the reporting begins; (2) The author characterizes federal agents as \"roaming\" and \"intimidating\" immigrants, \"violently\" confronting protesters, and bringing \"catastrophic results,\" signaling moral judgment rather than neutral description; (3) The piece claims agents and militias share a goal of \"relentless intimidation—of immigrants and the left\" and use \"militaristic tactics,\" aligning with Left-coded skepticism of law enforcement; (4) Central thesis asserts DHS has \"melded the views and tactics of the extremist fringe\" with federal power and that the White House created an \"in-house militia\" to \"harass\" opponents—strong ideological framing against the administration; (5) The author states both agents and militias are \"unpopular and out of step with the majority\" without presenting countervailing data in the author’s own voice, indicating selection/framing bias; (6) DOJ is portrayed as having \"done little\" to investigate and as focusing on blaming victims—negative evaluative framing of right-leaning institutions; (7) The author asserts \"nativist ideologies\" are driving policy, that the government is antagonizing communities of color, and that DHS is disseminating \"white-supremacist propaganda\"—claims aligned with Left concerns about systemic oppression and white supremacy; (8) Speculative warnings that these dynamics are \"sowing\" conditions for increased political violence and could lead to agents and militia members \"join[ing] forces\" heighten one-sided alarm; (9) While brief administration/DHS pushback is included, it is cursory and not integrated with substantive counter-argumentation, leaving the author’s thesis largely unchallenged; (10) Source check: The piece platforms Enrique Tarrio, longtime leader of the Proud Boys, a far-right extremist group."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report/analysis piece. It leans Left because the author repeatedly equates DHS/ICE actions with extremist militias, uses loaded descriptors to condemn federal enforcement, and advances a thesis that the Trump administration has effectively formed an "in-house militia" to harass opponents. The consistency of these value-laden framings, with limited balancing context, supports a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline and dek frame federal agents as emulating a "far-right" group, establishing a condemnatory premise before the reporting begins; (2) The author characterizes federal agents as "roaming" and "intimidating" immigrants, "violently" confronting protesters, and bringing "catastrophic results," signaling moral judgment rather than neutral description; (3) The piece claims agents and militias share a goal of "relentless intimidation—of immigrants and the left" and use "militaristic tactics," aligning with Left-coded skepticism of law enforcement; (4) Central thesis asserts DHS has "melded the views and tactics of the extremist fringe" with federal power and that the White House created an "in-house militia" to "harass" opponents—strong ideological framing against the administration; (5) The author states both agents and militias are "unpopular and out of step with the majority" without presenting countervailing data in the author’s own voice, indicating selection/framing bias; (6) DOJ is portrayed as having "done little" to investigate and as focusing on blaming victims—negative evaluative framing of right-leaning institutions; (7) The author asserts "nativist ideologies" are driving policy, that the government is antagonizing communities of color, and that DHS is disseminating "white-supremacist propaganda"—claims aligned with Left concerns about systemic oppression and white supremacy; (8) Speculative warnings that these dynamics are "sowing" conditions for increased political violence and could lead to agents and militia members "join[ing] forces" heighten one-sided alarm; (9) While brief administration/DHS pushback is included, it is cursory and not integrated with substantive counter-argumentation, leaving the author’s thesis largely unchallenged; (10) Source check: The piece platforms Enrique Tarrio, longtime leader of the Proud Boys, a far-right extremist group.
